key_points: Political institutions are bodies responsible for decision making in government
Institutions ensure decisions are made according to rules and procedures
The Mandal Commission recommended reservation for socially and educationally backward classes
The Indian Parliament is the supreme law making body
Parliament consists of the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Lok Sabha controls the executive through debates and no-confidence motions
Rajya Sabha represents states and reviews laws passed by Lok Sabha
The executive branch implements laws passed by the legislature
The Prime Minister is the leader of the government and the Council of Ministers
The Cabinet consists of senior ministers responsible for major decisions
Civil servants assist ministers in implementing government policies
The President performs functions according to the advice of the Council of Ministers
The judiciary is responsible for interpreting laws and protecting citizens' rights
The Supreme Court is the highest judicial authority in India
Judicial review allows courts to examine laws and ensure they follow the Constitution
examples: Example of executive authority: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
Example of judiciary authority: Supreme Court
Example of political decision: Mandal Commission implementation
Example of parliamentary control: No-confidence motion
Example of judicial power: Judicial review
Example of citizen legal action: Public Interest Litigation
formulas: Council of Ministers → Responsible to Lok Sabha
Judicial review → Courts check constitutionality of laws
exam_tips: Remember the three branches of government: Legislature, Executive and Judiciary
Lok Sabha has greater powers in financial matters
Rajya Sabha is a permanent house
Prime Minister is the most powerful executive authority
Civil servants belong to the permanent executive
Supreme Court protects the Constitution and citizens' rights
Judicial review ensures laws follow constitutional principles
Institutions distribute power and prevent misuse of authority
